key_insight: Mizzle gives LLM-based bug finding a machine-checkable proof obligation, so only genuinely reachable bugs can be reported as true positives.
engineering_relevance: This can reduce false alarms in agentic vulnerability and correctness analysis for concurrent OCaml systems by requiring certified bug reports instead of free-form claims.
method: Build an incorrectness separation logic for a substantial concurrent OCaml subset.
Mechanize the logic in Rocq on top of Iris so proofs are checkable.
Prove soundness and completeness, then instantiate it for stuckness, non-linearizability, and races.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a proof-of-concept showing that an LLM can use Mizzle to certify the existence of a bug, alongside mechanized soundness and completeness proofs.
watch_item: The approach depends on substantial mechanization and program-logic integration, so practical adoption may be limited by proof engineering cost and language-coverage boundaries.
paper_title: Mizzle: A Complete Concurrent Incorrectness Logic for Preventing False Alarms in Agentic Bug Finding
